office:text.text:p: AI Finance Automation Tool for Purchase Invoices and Bank Statement Matching
Borek Finance currently manages purchase invoices manually in an Excel file calledPurchase Invoices Database.
The goal of this project is to build an AI-powered internal tool that can:

text:list-item.text:p: Read invoices from uploaded files, PDFs, scanned documents, or photos.
Extract the required invoice information automatically.
Save the extracted data into the correct columns of thePurchase Invoices Database Excel.
Detect invoice numbers inside the bank statementComment / Komenticolumn.
Match those invoice numbers with theInvoice Numbercolumn in the Purchase Invoices Database.
Automatically fill thepaid at (Date)column in the Purchase Invoices Database.
Leave thepaid bycolumn manual for the Finance team.

office:text.text:p: The system should help Finance reduce repetitive work, but it should not fully replace human review. If the AI is unsure, the row must be marked for manual checking.
2. Main Files Used in the Process
This is the main Excel file where extracted invoice data will be saved.

table:table-cell.text:p: Related client or employee, if visible

office:text.text:p: Important:ThePurchase Invoices Databaseis the main output file/database of the system.
This file is uploaded separately by Finance.
Important columns from the bank statement:

table:table-cell.text:p: Tipi i veprimit / Type of transaction

office:text.text:p: The most important column for matching is:
The system must detect invoice numbers from this comment field.

1. Finance uploads invoice files:- PDF- scanned PDF- photo- image- digital invoice2. AI reads each invoice.3. AI extracts:- invoice date- company name- company address- invoice number- amount- account details- description- possible category4. Extracted data is saved into the Purchase Invoices Database.5. Finance uploads Bank Statement Excel.6. System reads the bank statement rows.7. System checks the Komenti / Comment column.8. AI or regex detects invoice numbers inside the comment text.9. System compares detected invoice numbers with Invoice Number in Purchase Invoices Database.10. If a match is found:- take Data / Date from Bank Statement- paste it into paid at (Date) in Purchase Invoices Database11. paid by remains empty/manual.12. If no match is found:- mark row as Needs Review.
The system must allow users to upload one or multiple invoice files.
The upload page should allow batch upload because Finance may upload many invoices at once.
Each uploaded file should be stored with metadata:
The AI must extract structured data from every invoice.
The AI should not return only free text.It must return structured JSON so the backend can save it correctly.
After extraction, the system must insert a new row into the Purchase Invoices Database.
paid at (Date) should NOT be filled from invoice extraction.paid at (Date) should be filled only from the bank statement matching process.paid by should remain manual.
The system must allow the Finance team to upload the Bank Statement Excel file.
The backend must read the bank statement and identify the header row.
From the uploaded example, the relevant bank statement headers include:
Data / DateVlera e debituar / Debited amountVlera e kredituar / Credited amountTipi i veprimit / Type of transactionKomenti / Comment
The system must process each transaction row and store:
Invoice numbers can appear in different formats inside the bank statement comment.
The system should detect patterns like:
The interns should implement a function like:
def extract_invoice_numbers(comment: str) -> list[str]:"""Detect possible invoice numbers from bank statement comment text.Return a list of normalized invoice number candidates."""
The system should normalize invoice numbers before matching.
First, the system should try exact invoice number matching.
Bank Statement Comment:"Payment for invoice 26063"Detected invoice number:26063Purchase Invoices Database:Invoice Number = 26063Result:Match foundpaid at (Date) = Bank Statement Data / Date
6.2 Multiple Invoice Numbers in One Comment
Sometimes one bank transaction may include more than one invoice number.
The system should detect all invoice numbers:
Then it should update all matching rows in the Purchase Invoices Database with the same payment date.
If multiple invoices are paid in one batch, the system should support this logic:

One bank transaction can pay multiple invoices.If the comment contains several invoice numbers, update all matching invoices.If the comment does not clearly contain all invoice numbers, mark as Needs Review.
For MVP, do not overcomplicate the batch logic.
If invoice numbers are visible in the comment, match them.If invoice numbers are not visible, do not guess.Send to manual review.
Use amount combination matching:Sum of several unpaid invoices = debited bank amountSame supplierSame date rangeThen suggest a possible batch match
If no invoice number from the bank statement comment matches the Purchase Invoices Database, the system should mark it as:
Invoice number not uploaded yetInvoice number written differentlyComment does not contain invoice numberInvoice belongs to another projectManual finance case
The paid date update is one of the most important features.

for transaction in bank_statement_rows:comment = transaction["comment"]transaction_date = transaction["date"]invoice_numbers = extract_invoice_numbers(comment)for invoice_number in invoice_numbers:matched_invoice = find_invoice_by_number(invoice_number)if matched_invoice:matched_invoice["paid at (Date)"] = transaction_datematched_invoice["match_status"] = "Matched"else:create_review_task(transaction, invoice_number)
Only paid at (Date) is updated automatically.paid by remains manual.
The system must not blindly trust AI output.
Each invoice extraction should have a confidence score.

table:table-cell.text:p: Do not finalize, require manual review

office:text.text:p: A row should be marked asNeeds Reviewwhen:
Invoice number is missingAmount is missingCompany name is unclearInvoice date is unclearBank statement match failedMultiple possible matches foundAI confidence is low
DashboardUpload InvoicesUpload Bank StatementPurchase Invoices TableBank Transactions TableMatching ResultsManual Review QueueExport Page
Even if the output remains Excel, a database is recommended because it is safer and easier to manage.
The Excel file can be generated/exported from the database.
For invoice extraction, interns can test two options.
Option A: OpenAI Vision / GPT model
Use it to read invoice images/PDFs and extract JSON.
Different invoice layoutsPhotosScanned invoicesFlexible field extractionValidation
Option B: Google Document AI Invoice Parser
OCR / Document parser extracts raw text↓OpenAI validates and converts result to clean JSON↓Backend saves JSON into database/Excel

{"id": 1,"invoice_date": "2026-05-15","name_of_company": "ADIA Group","address_of_company": "Prishtina, Kosovo","invoice_number": "26063","amount": 220.66,"currency": "EUR","account_details": "IBAN...","internal_note_description": "Water supply invoice","client_employee_related": "","paid_at_date": null,"paid_by": null,"fixed_status": "","category": "Utilities","extraction_confidence": 0.94,"review_status": "Approved","source_file": "adia_invoice.pdf"}
Drag-and-drop uploadMultiple file uploadFile statusAI extraction statusError message if extraction failsReview button
Invoice DateName of CompanyAddressInvoice NumberAmountAccount DetailsDescriptionClient / Employee Relatedpaid at (Date)paid byFixed/Not fixedCategoryReview StatusMatch Status
The user should be able to edit fields manually.
Upload Excel filePreview extracted rowsShow detected invoice numbers from Comment columnShow processing status
Matched invoicesUnmatched invoicesUnmatched bank transactionsNeeds Review casesBatch payment candidates
Original invoice file previewAI extracted dataEditable fieldsBank transaction candidateReason for reviewApprove buttonReject buttonSave correction button
Missing invoice numberLow AI confidenceNo bank statement matchMultiple possible matchesAmount mismatchUnclear scanned invoice
The system must export the updated Purchase Invoices Database back to Excel.
The exported Excel must keep the same column structure:
Invoice DateName of CompanyAdress of CompanyInvoice NumberAmountAccount DetailsInternal Note/DescriptionClient / Employee Relatedpaid at (Date)paid byFixed/Not fixedCategory
Optional additional columns for internal system use:
But for Finance’s official file, keep the original columns unless they approve extra columns.
Because this system processes financial data, interns must follow these rules:

Do not connect directly to bank accounts.Use only uploaded bank statement files.Do not expose uploaded invoices publicly.Store files securely.Add authentication.Add role-based access.Log all edits.Log all AI extractions.Log all payment date updates.Keep original files for audit.Allow manual correction.
Upload invoice filesExtract invoice fields with AISave extracted dataDisplay invoice tableExport Purchase Invoices Excel
Upload bank statement ExcelRead transaction rowsExtract Date, Amount, CommentDetect invoice numbers from CommentDisplay bank transaction table
Match detected invoice numbers with Purchase Invoices DatabaseFill paid at (Date)Leave paid by manualShow matched and unmatched rowsCreate review queue
Review uncertain AI extractionsEdit extracted invoice dataApprove or reject matchesTrack review status
Monthly invoice totalPaid/unpaid invoicesUnmatched invoicesCategory breakdownExport report
FastAPI setupInvoice upload endpointBank statement upload endpointExcel reading/writingDatabase modelsExport to Excel
Invoice OCR/AI extractionPrompt design for JSON outputConfidence scoringInvoice field validationHandling photos and scanned PDFs
Invoice number parserBank comment parserMatching algorithmReact upload pagesInvoice tableReview queue UI
If there are only two interns:
Intern 1: Backend, database, Excel, matchingIntern 2: AI extraction, frontend, review UI

Finance can upload invoice files.AI extracts invoice data.Data appears in the Purchase Invoices Database table.Finance can upload Bank Statement Excel.System reads the Comment column.System detects invoice numbers from comments.System matches invoice numbers with Purchase Invoices Database.System fills paid at (Date) automatically.paid by remains manual.Unmatched cases are shown in Needs Review.Finance can export the updated Purchase Invoices Excel.
The system should follow this principle:
AI can extract and suggest.The system can auto-fill when there is a clear match.Finance must review unclear cases.Never guess financial data when confidence is low.
The most important matching rule is:
Bank Statement Komenti / Comment contains invoice number↓Find same invoice number in Purchase Invoices Database↓Copy Bank Statement Data / Date↓Paste into Purchase Invoices paid at (Date)
This is the core logic of the project.
